channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Researchers in limbo as Columbia bows to Trump’s demands in bid to restore $400M federal funding
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When President Donald Trump canceled $400 million in funding to Columbia University over its handling of student protests against Israel's war in Gaza, much of the financial pain fell on researchers a train ride away from the school's campus, working on things like curing cancer and
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Signal chat isn't 'really an FBI thing.' The FBI has a long history of such inquiries
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel may not have been part of a Signal chat in which other Trump administration national security officials discussed detailed attack plans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE slashes billions more in expenses for programs like Peruvian climate change and gender equity in Mexico
item.description: DOGE announced it slashed 113 contracts valued at $4.7 billion for $3.3 billion in savings for programs like climate change in Peru and gender equity in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge denies Trump admin's effort to ban transgender people from military
item.description: A federal judge denied the Department of Defense's effort to block transgender people from enlisting in the U.S. military under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congressional Democrats find their voice as Signal slip-up becomes talk of the town
item.description: Democrats took the opportunity to drill into CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard during "worldwide threats" hearings this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration struggled Wednesday to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, STEPHEN GROVES, and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: Democrats say the list originated from the desk of Peter Marocco, one of the leading figures dismantling USAID alongside Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging that his prosecution be moved out of New York state court so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A bill proposing a near-total abortion ban causes an uproar at Georgia's Capitol
item.description: Protesters on each side of the abortion debate have packed a hallway of Georgia's Capitol during a hearing on a bill that would ban most abortions.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: A federal appeals court won’t lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NRA legislative expert says gun rights could see 'most monumental' win in Congress since 2005
item.description: Two major gun rights bills are currently advancing their way through Congress, including legislation seeking to create nationwide reciprocity for concealed carry permits, something the NRA says would be the "most monumental" win for Second Amendment rights in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fani Willis 'thinks she's above the law,' says Georgia lawmaker in subpoena fight
item.description: Georgia lawmakers deliberated Wednesday on how to press Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to comply with a committee subpoena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep Jasmine Crockett refuses to apologize for 'Gov Hot Wheels' comment
item.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett stormed away from an interview with FOX Business after being asked about calling Gov. Greg Abbott "Governor Hot Wheels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE says it needs to know the government’s most sensitive data, but can’t say why
item.description: DOGE staffers have skirted privacy laws, training and security protocols to gain virtually unfettered access to financial and personal information stored in siloed government databases.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump officials texted war plans to a group chat in a secure app that included a journalist
item.description: Top national security officials for President Donald Trump, including his defense secretary, texted war plans for upcoming military strikes in Yemen to a group chat in a secure messaging app that included the editor-in-chief for The Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
